output.final_deliverable:
Answer:
- The capital city of Australia is Canberra.

Authoritative source (one URL) and verbatim evidence:
- Source: "Australia | History, Cities, Population, Capital, Map, & Facts | Britannica" — https://www.britannica.com/place/Australia
- Verbatim excerpt from the search result metadata for that page: "Its capital city is Canberra" (source: https://www.britannica.com/place/Australia).

Note about raw page text availability:
- The RAW VISIT CONTENT (full page text from visited URLs) was not provided in the data set, so I cannot include a verbatim excerpt extracted from a downloaded page file. The search results returned an authoritative Britannica page that explicitly states the capital; the exact quoted phrase above is taken from the merged search result metadata returned in the task data (see the cited Britannica URL).
output.action_summary: The capital of Australia is Canberra. Cited authoritative source: Encyclopaedia Britannica — https://www.britannica.com/place/Australia (verbatim excerpt from search metadata: "Its capital city is Canberra").

merged_results.title: Search for authoritative sources that state the capital of Australia

result.query: capital city of Australia official site Canberra site:.gov.au OR site:.edu OR site:.org OR site:britannica.com
result.intent: Find authoritative, citable webpages (Australian government, reputable encyclopedias, or educational institutions) that explicitly state the capital of Australia.

results.title: Canberra | History, Map, Population, Climate, & Facts | Britannica
results.description: Canberra, federal capital of the Commonwealth of Australia. It occupies part of the Australian Capital Territory, in southern Australia, and is about 150 miles (240 km) southwest of Sydney.

results.title: Australian Capital Territory (A.C.T.) | Flag, Facts, Maps, & Points of Interest | Britannica
results.description: Australian Parliament House; Museum of Australian Democracy at Old Parliament HouseThe Australian Parliament House (top) and the Museum of Australian Democracy at Old Parliament House (bottom), Canberra, Austl.(more) ... Australian Capital Territory (A.C.T.), political entity of the Commonwealth of Australia consisting of Canberra, the national and territorial capital, and surrounding land.

results.title: Australia | History, Cities, Population, Capital, Map, & Facts | Britannica
results.description: Australia is the smallest continent and one of the largest countries on Earth, lying between the Pacific and Indian oceans in the Southern Hemisphere. Its capital city is Canberra, and its most important economic and cultural centers are Sydney ...

results.title: Canberra - Kids | Britannica Kids | Homework Help
results.description: Canberra is the capital of the country of Australia. The city is also the center of a district called the Australian Capital Territory. Canberra lies at the foot of the Australian Alps mountain range. The Molonglo River flows through the city.

results.title: Australian Capital Territory - History, Settlement, Landscape | Britannica
results.description: In 1908 it was determined that the capital would be in the Canberra-Yass district, not less than 900 square miles (2,330 square km) in area, and have access to the sea. The federal government began acquiring the land for the city in 1911. The land has remained in government ownership in accordance with the Seat of Government (Administration) Act of 1910. Also in 1911, the Commonwealth of Australia launched an international competition for the design of its new capital.

details.goal_evaluation: Not achieved. While multiple authoritative search results explicitly state that Canberra is the capital of Australia (satisfying factual identification), the original deliverable required a concise answer plus a single authoritative source URL with a short citation line and an extracted supporting quote. That citation and quote were not produced because the visit to the intended government site failed and the synthesis step did not emit a final citation. To meet the goal fully, provide a retrieved/citable URL and an extracted excerpt (and retrieval metadata) from an authoritative page.
missing_requirements: A single authoritative source URL retrieved and cited (e.g., an official government page or a stable encyclopedia page) that can be linked.
An extracted supporting quote from that retrieved page that explicitly names Canberra as the capital.
A short citation line (page title, publisher, retrieval date) for the chosen source.

If the government site is preferred, resolve the HTTP 403 (use alternative gov.au endpoint, set appropriate headers, or use another official source such as australia.gov.au or the Parliament of Australia site).

synthesized.summary: Searches located multiple reputable sources (Britannica entries) that explicitly state Canberra is Australia’s capital. An attempt to fetch an official ACT/government page (act.gov.au) failed with an HTTP 403, and the final synthesis step did not produce the required single citation + extracted quote. Therefore the objective (answer + one authoritative cited URL with excerpt) is only partially complete: the answer is supported by search results, but the deliverable citation/excerpt was not produced.
key_findings: Authoritative encyclopedic evidence: Britannica explicitly names Canberra as the capital. Examples found: https://www.britannica.com/place/Canberra ("federal capital of the Commonwealth of Australia") and https://www.britannica.com/place/Australia ("Its capital city is Canberra"). (Source: node 1d13ecbd-f230-4998-b554-5367e0e1dd08 search results.)

Additional Britannica pages also confirm Canberra is the national/territorial capital and describe the Australian Capital Territory as containing the capital. (Several Britannica links returned in the search results — node 1d13ecbd-f230-4998-b554-5367e0e1dd08.)

Official site fetch failed: attempted visit to an ACT/government URL (act.gov.au) returned HTTP 403 Forbidden and could not be retrieved. (Error from node 7ffab5b4-d6fe-46d6-9580-bf6e6078d851: "HTTP fetch failed: https://www.act.gov.au status=403".)
Synthesis step planned to produce final answer + single citation but did not output a citation or extracted quote — it only recorded the intent/plan. (node dbdc4a22-0742-4636-b1fd-8fdc9fac589b).
synthesized.goal_evaluation: Not achieved. That citation and quote were not produced because the visit to the intended government site failed and the synthesis step did not emit a final citation. To meet the goal fully, provide a retrieved/citable URL and an extracted excerpt (and retrieval metadata) from an authoritative page.
missing_requirements: A single authoritative source URL retrieved and cited (e.g., an official government page or a stable encyclopedia page) that can be linked.
An extracted supporting quote from that retrieved page that explicitly names Canberra as the capital.
A short citation line (page title, publisher, retrieval date) for the chosen source.

If the government site is preferred, resolve the HTTP 403 (use alternative gov.au endpoint, set appropriate headers, or use another official source such as australia.gov.au or the Parliament of Australia site).
